```{R}
                                measure.vars = c("Neg_Assoc", "Pos_Assoc"), 
                                variable.name = "Assoc_Type", 
                                value.name = "Count")
  assoc_data_long_pheno[Assoc_Type == "Neg_Assoc", Count := -Count]
  assoc_data_long_pheno[, Assoc_Type := factor(Assoc_Type, levels = c("Neg_Assoc", "Pos_Assoc"), 
                                               labels = c("Negative", "Positive"))]
  assoc_data_long_pheno[, Index_Type := "Associations"]
  
  plot_data_combined_pheno <- rbind(
    plot_data_long_pheno[, .(var_description, Index_Type, Value = Index_Value, Assoc_Type)],
    assoc_data_long_pheno[, .(var_description, Index_Type, Value = Count, Assoc_Type)]
  )
  
  plot_data_combined_pheno[, Index_Type := factor(Index_Type, 
                                                  levels = c("Stain", "Oxygen", "Motility", "Sporulation", "Associations"))]
  y_order_pheno <- assoc_data_pheno[, .(var_description, Total_Assoc)][order(Total_Assoc, desc(var_description))]$var_description
  plot_data_combined_pheno[, var_description := factor(var_description, levels = y_order_pheno)]
  
  mean_data_pheno <- plot_data_combined_pheno[Index_Type != "Associations", 
                                              .(Mean_Value = mean(Value, na.rm = TRUE)), 
                                              by = .(var_description, Index_Type, Assoc_Type)]
  
  assoc_max_pheno <- ceiling(max(abs(assoc_data_long_pheno$Count), na.rm = TRUE) / 5) * 5
  assoc_breaks_pheno <- seq(-assoc_max_pheno, assoc_max_pheno, by = 5)
  
  p_pheno <- ggplot(plot_data_combined_pheno, aes(x = Value, y = var_description)) +
    geom_violin(data = plot_data_combined_pheno[Index_Type != "Associations"], 
                aes(fill = Assoc_Type), alpha = 0.5, color = "black", size = 0.1, na.rm = TRUE, 
                width = 0.8, scale = "width", position = "identity") +
    geom_point(data = mean_data_pheno,
               aes(x = Mean_Value, y = var_description, color = Assoc_Type, fill = Assoc_Type),
               shape = 21, size = 2, stroke = 0.4, na.rm = TRUE) +
    geom_bar(data = plot_data_combined_pheno[Index_Type == "Associations"], 
             aes(fill = Assoc_Type), stat = "identity", width = 0.8) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Negative" = "#D55E00", "Positive" = "#0072B2"), na.translate = FALSE) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Negative" = "#D55E00", "Positive" = "#0072B2"), na.translate = FALSE) +
    geom_vline(aes(xintercept = ifelse(Index_Type == "Associations", 0, 0.5)), 
               linetype = "dashed", color = "black", size = 0.2) +
    facet_wrap(~ Index_Type, nrow = 1, scales = "free_x") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = function(x) {
      if (max(x, na.rm = TRUE) <= 1) {
        c(0, 0.5, 1)
      } else {
        assoc_breaks_pheno
      }
    }, labels = function(x) x) +
    labs(title = "Indices and Significant Associations by Phenotype/Exposure",
         x = NULL,
         y = "Phenotype/Exposure Description") +
    theme_article() +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 6),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 6, hjust = 0.5),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 6),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 6),
      strip.text = element_text(size = 6, angle = 90, hjust = 0)
    )
  
  file_name_pheno <- file.path(output_dir, paste0(file_prefix, "_combined.pdf"))
  save_pdf_figure(file_name_pheno, plot_obj = p_pheno, width_mm = width_mm, height_mm = height_mm)
  
  return(p_pheno)
}

# Function for OTU plots
plot_otu_indices <- function(mapping_data, input, file_prefix = "16.1_indices", output_dir = viz_out_path, 
                             width_mm = 80, height_mm = 170, prefix_subsets = NULL, prefixes_key = NULL) {
  setDT(input)
  setDT(mapping_data)
  
  if (!is.null(prefix_subsets)) {
    pheno_col <- if ("phenotype" %in% names(input)) "phenotype" else "exposure"
    input <- input[substr(get(pheno_col), 1, 3) %in% prefix_subsets]
  }
  
  pheno_col <- if ("phenotype" %in% names(input)) "phenotype" else "exposure"
  
  unique_otus <- unique(input$otu)
  plot_data_otu <- merge(input[, .(otu, estimate, pheno = get(pheno_col))], 
                         mapping_data[otu %in% unique_otus, .(otu, parsed_genus, STAIN_INDEX, OXYGEN_INDEX, MOTILITY_INDEX, SPORULATION_INDEX)], 
                         by = "otu", all.x = TRUE)
  
  dummy_row <- data.table(otu = "DUMMY_OTU", estimate = 0, pheno = "DUMMY_empty_row_for_padding_placeholder_DUMMY", 
                          parsed_genus = "DUMMY_empty_row_for_padding_placeholder_DUMMY", 
                          STAIN_INDEX = NA_real_, OXYGEN_INDEX = NA_real_, MOTILITY_INDEX = NA_real_, SPORULATION_INDEX = NA_real_)
  plot_data_otu <- rbind(plot_data_otu, dummy_row)
  
  plot_data_long_otu <- melt(plot_data_otu, 
                             id.vars = c("otu", "parsed_genus", "estimate", "pheno"), 
                             measure.vars = c("STAIN_INDEX", "OXYGEN_INDEX", "MOTILITY_INDEX", "SPORULATION_INDEX"),
                             variable.name = "Index_Type", 
                             value.name = "Index_Value")
  
  plot_data_long_otu[, Index_Type := factor(Index_Type, 
                                            levels = c("STAIN_INDEX", "OXYGEN_INDEX", "MOTILITY_INDEX", "SPORULATION_INDEX"),
                                            labels = c("Stain", "Oxygen", "Motility", "Sporulation"))]
  plot_data_long_otu[, Index_Value := as.numeric(Index_Value)]
  plot_data_long_otu[, Assoc_Type := factor(ifelse(estimate < 0, "Negative", "Positive"), levels = c("Negative", "Positive"))]
  
  plot_data_long_otu <- plot_data_long_otu[, {
    n_valid <- sum(!is.na(Index_Value))
    if (n_valid == 0) {
      data.table(Index_Value = c(0.5 - 0.01, 0.5 + 0.01), 
                 Assoc_Type = factor(c("Negative", "Positive"), levels = c("Negative", "Positive")))
    } else if (n_valid == 1) {
      val <- Index_Value[!is.na(Index_Value)]
      assoc <- Assoc_Type[!is.na(Index_Value)]
      data.table(Index_Value = c(val, val - 0.01, val + 0.01), 
                 Assoc_Type = factor(c(assoc, "Negative", "Positive"), levels = c("Negative", "Positive")))
    } else {
      data.table(Index_Value = Index_Value, Assoc_Type = Assoc_Type)
    }
  }, by = .(parsed_genus, Index_Type)]
  
  assoc_data_otu <- input[, .(Neg_Assoc = sum(estimate < 0, na.rm = TRUE), 
                              Pos_Assoc = sum(estimate > 0, na.rm = TRUE)), 
                          by = otu]
  dummy_assoc <- data.table(otu = "DUMMY_OTU", Neg_Assoc = 0, Pos_Assoc = 0)
  assoc_data_otu <- rbind(assoc_data_otu, dummy_assoc)
  assoc_data_otu <- merge(assoc_data_otu, mapping_data[, .(otu, parsed_genus)], by = "otu", all.x = TRUE)
  assoc_data_otu[otu == "DUMMY_OTU", parsed_genus := "DUMMY_empty_row_for_padding_placeholder_DUMMY"]
  assoc_data_otu <- assoc_data_otu[!duplicated(parsed_genus)]
  assoc_data_otu[, Total_Assoc := Neg_Assoc + Pos_Assoc]
  assoc_data_otu <- assoc_data_otu[order(Total_Assoc, desc(parsed_genus))]
  
  assoc_data_long_otu <- melt(assoc_data_otu, 
                              id.vars = "parsed_genus", 
                              measure.vars = c("Neg_Assoc", "Pos_Assoc"), 
                              variable.name = "Assoc_Type", 
                              value.name = "Count")
  assoc_data_long_otu[Assoc_Type == "Neg_Assoc", Count := -Count]
  assoc_data_long_otu[, Assoc_Type := factor(Assoc_Type, levels = c("Neg_Assoc", "Pos_Assoc"), 
                                             labels = c("Negative", "Positive"))]
  assoc_data_long_otu[, Index_Type := "Associations"]
  
  plot_data_combined_otu <- rbind(
    plot_data_long_otu[, .(parsed_genus, Index_Type, Value = Index_Value, Assoc_Type)],
    assoc_data_long_otu[, .(parsed_genus, Index_Type, Value = Count, Assoc_Type)]
  )
  
  plot_data_combined_otu[, Index_Type := factor(Index_Type, 
                                                levels = c("Stain", "Oxygen", "Motility", "Sporulation", "Associations"))]
  y_order_otu <- assoc_data_otu[, .(parsed_genus, Total_Assoc)][order(Total_Assoc, desc(parsed_genus))]$parsed_genus
  plot_data_combined_otu[, parsed_genus := factor(parsed_genus, levels = y_order_otu)]
  
  mean_data_otu <- plot_data_combined_otu[Index_Type != "Associations", 
                                          .(Mean_Value = mean(Value, na.rm = TRUE)), 
                                          by = .(parsed_genus, Index_Type, Assoc_Type)]
  
  assoc_max_otu <- ceiling(max(abs(assoc_data_long_otu$Count), na.rm = TRUE) / 5) * 5
  assoc_breaks_otu <- seq(-assoc_max_otu, assoc_max_otu, by = 5)
  
  p_otu <- ggplot(plot_data_combined_otu, aes(x = Value, y = parsed_genus)) +
    geom_violin(data = plot_data_combined_otu[Index_Type != "Associations"], 
                aes(fill = Assoc_Type), alpha = 0.5, color = "black", size = 0.1, na.rm = TRUE, 
                width = 0.8, scale = "width", position = "identity") +
    geom_point(data = mean_data_otu,  
               aes(x = Mean_Value, y = parsed_genus, color = Assoc_Type, fill = Assoc_Type),  
               shape = 21, size = 2, stroke = 0.4, na.rm = TRUE) +
    geom_bar(data = plot_data_combined_otu[Index_Type == "Associations"], 
             aes(fill = Assoc_Type), stat = "identity", width = 0.8) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c("Negative" = "#D55E00", "Positive" = "#0072B2"), na.translate = FALSE) +
    scale_color_manual(values = c("Negative" = "#D55E00", "Positive" = "#0072B2"), na.translate = FALSE) +
    geom_vline(aes(xintercept = ifelse(Index_Type == "Associations", 0, 0.5)), 
               linetype = "dashed", color = "black", size = 0.2) +
    facet_wrap(~ Index_Type, nrow = 1, scales = "free_x") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = function(x) {
      if (max(x, na.rm = TRUE) <= 1) {
        c(0, 0.5, 1)
      } else {
        assoc_breaks_otu
      }
    }, labels = function(x) x) +
    labs(title = "Indices and Significant Associations by OTU",
         x = NULL,
         y = "Genus") +
    theme_article() +
    theme(
      legend.position = "bottom",
      legend.title = element_blank(),
      legend.text = element_text(size = 6),
      plot.title = element_text(size = 6, hjust = 0.5),
      axis.title = element_text(size = 6),
      axis.text = element_text(size = 6),
      strip.text = element_text(size = 6, angle = 90, hjust = 0)
    )
  
  file_name_otu <- file.path(output_dir, paste0(file_prefix, "_otu_combined.pdf"))
  save_pdf_figure(file_name_otu, plot_obj = p_otu, width_mm = width_mm, height_mm = height_mm)
  
  return(p_otu)
}

# Example usage with real dataframes including prefix subsets
dataframes <- list(
  # # demowas_sig_res
  # list(data = demowas_sig_res, prefix = "16.1_demowas_indices", 
  #      width_mm_pheno = 100, height_mm_pheno = 70, width_mm_otu = 100, height_mm_otu = 190),
  # list(data = demowas_sig_res, prefix = "16.1_demowas_indices_AGE_SQUARED_RIDAGEYR", 
  #      width_mm_pheno = 100, height_mm_pheno = 40, width_mm_otu = 100, height_mm_otu = 40,
  #      prefix_subsets = c("AGE_SQUARED", "RIDAGEYR"), prefixes_key = "AGE_SQUARED_RIDAGEYR"),
  # 
  # # oral_d_sig_res (no prefix filter)
  # list(data = oral_d_sig_res, prefix = "16.2_oral_d_indices", 
  #      width_mm_pheno = 100, height_mm_pheno = 55, width_mm_otu = 100, height_mm_otu = 140),
  # 
  # # phewas_sig_res
  # list(data = phewas_sig_res, prefix = "16.3_phewas_indices", 
  #      width_mm_pheno = 100, height_mm_pheno = 270, width_mm_otu = 100, height_mm_otu = 210),
  # list(data = phewas_sig_res, prefix = "16.3_phewas_indices_LBX", 
  #      width_mm_pheno = 100, height_mm_pheno = 200, width_mm_otu = 100, height_mm_otu = 180,
  #      prefix_subsets = c("LBX"), prefixes_key = "LBX"),
  # list(data = phewas_sig_res, prefix = "16.3_phewas_indices_LBD_URX_ENX", 
  #      width_mm_pheno = 100, height_mm_pheno = 150, width_mm_otu = 100, height_mm_otu = 140,
  #      prefix_subsets = c("LBD", "URX", "ENX"), prefixes_key = "LBD_URX_ENX"),
  # list(data = phewas_sig_res, prefix = "16.3_phewas_indices_BMX_BPX_MDI_MSY_SPX", 
  #      width_mm_pheno = 100, height_mm_pheno = 180, width_mm_otu = 100, height_mm_otu = 160,
  #      prefix_subsets = c("BMX", "BPX", "MDI", "MSY", "SPX"), prefixes_key = "BMX_BPX_MDI_MSY_SPX"),
  # 
  # # exwas_sig_res
  # list(data = exwas_sig_res, prefix = "16.4_exwas_indices", 
  #      width_mm_pheno = 100, height_mm_pheno = 220, width_mm_otu = 100, height_mm_otu = 210),
  # list(data = exwas_sig_res, prefix = "16.4_exwas_indices_DR1_DR2", 
  #      width_mm_pheno = 100, height_mm_pheno = 120, width_mm_otu = 100, height_mm_otu = 110,
  #      prefix_subsets = c("DR1", "DR2"), prefixes_key = "DR1_DR2"),
  # list(data = exwas_sig_res, prefix = "16.4_exwas_indices_DS1_DS2_SMQ", 
  #      width_mm_pheno = 100, height_mm_pheno = 130, width_mm_otu = 100, height_mm_otu = 120,
  #      prefix_subsets = c("DS1", "DS2", "SMQ"), prefixes_key = "DS1_DS2_SMQ"),
  # list(data = exwas_sig_res, prefix = "16.4_exwas_indices_LBX", 
  #      width_mm_pheno = 100, height_mm_pheno = 200, width_mm_otu = 100, height_mm_otu = 180,
  #      prefix_subsets = c("LBX"), prefixes_key = "LBX"),
  # 
  # outwas_sig_res
  list(data = outwas_sig_res, prefix = "16.5_outwas_indices", 
       width_mm_pheno = 100, height_mm_pheno = 65, width_mm_otu = 100, height_mm_otu = 125),
  list(data = outwas_sig_res, prefix = "16.5_outwas_indices_CAN", 
       width_mm_pheno = 100, height_mm_pheno = 50, width_mm_otu = 100, height_mm_otu = 60,
       prefix_subsets = c("CAN"), prefixes_key = "CAN"),
  list(data = outwas_sig_res, prefix = "16.5_outwas_indices_CHD_HEA_ANG_STR", 
       width_mm_pheno = 100, height_mm_pheno = 55, width_mm_otu = 100, height_mm_otu = 110,
       prefix_subsets = c("CHD", "HEA", "ANG", "STR"), prefixes_key = "CHD_HEA_ANG_STR"),
  list(data = outwas_sig_res, prefix = "16.5_outwas_indices_EMP_AST_BRO", 
       width_mm_pheno = 100, height_mm_pheno = 50, width_mm_otu = 100, height_mm_otu = 100,
       prefix_subsets = c("EMP", "AST", "BRO"), prefixes_key = "EMP_AST_BRO"),
  list(data = outwas_sig_res, prefix = "16.5_outwas_indices_DIA_CVD", 
       width_mm_pheno = 100, height_mm_pheno = 45, width_mm_otu = 100, height_mm_otu = 90,
       prefix_subsets = c("DIA", "CVD"), prefixes_key = "DIA_CVD")
)

for (df in dataframes) {
  plot_pheno_indices(ubiome_genus_mapping_complete, df$data, file_prefix = df$prefix, 
                     width_mm = df$width_mm_pheno, height_mm = df$height_mm_pheno,
                     prefix_subsets = df$prefix_subsets, prefixes_key = df$prefixes_key)
  
  plot_otu_indices(ubiome_genus_mapping_complete, df$data, file_prefix = df$prefix, 
                   width_mm = df$width_mm_otu, height_mm = df$height_mm_otu,
                   prefix_subsets = df$prefix_subsets, prefixes_key = df$prefixes_key)
}
```

```{R}
# 
# # Additional example usage cases for future users with smaller data subsets
# # Example 1: Subset phewas_sig_res for specific phenotypes
# 
# subset_phewas <- phewas_sig_res[phenotype %in% c("SPXNFEV1", "LBXSLDSI"), ]
# plot_pheno_indices(ubiome_genus_mapping_complete, subset_phewas, file_prefix = "16.2_phewas_subset_pheno", 
#                    width_mm = 100, height_mm = 200)
# plot_otu_indices(ubiome_genus_mapping_complete, subset_phewas, file_prefix = "16.2_phewas_subset_otu", 
#                  width_mm = 110, height_mm = 210)
# 
# # Example 2: Subset exwas_sig_res for top 10 exposures by p-value
# subset_exwas <- exwas_sig_res[order(p.value.fdr)][1:10, ]
# plot_pheno_indices(ubiome_genus_mapping_complete, subset_exwas, file_prefix = "16.2_exwas_top10_pheno", 
#                    width_mm = 150, height_mm = 300)
# plot_otu_indices(ubiome_genus_mapping_complete, subset_exwas, file_prefix = "16.2_exwas_top10_otu", 
#                  width_mm = 160, height_mm = 310)
# 
# # Example 3: Subset outwas_sig_res for specific OTUs
# subset_outwas <- outwas_sig_res[otu %in% c("RSV_genus1320_relative", "RSV_genus1307_relative"), ]
# plot_pheno_indices(ubiome_genus_mapping_complete, subset_outwas, file_prefix = "16.2_outwas_subset_pheno", 
#                    width_mm = 90, height_mm = 180)
# plot_otu_indices(ubiome_genus_mapping_complete, subset_outwas, file_prefix = "16.2_outwas_subset_otu",  
#                  width_mm = 95, height_mm = 190)

# PDF file paths
pdf_paths <- c(
  "/Users/byeongyeoncho/Desktop/oral_ubiome/nhanes_ubiome_cho/0_manuscript_phyloseq_ubiome/2_figures_out/16.1_demowas_indices_otu_combined.pdf",
  "/Users/byeongyeoncho/Desktop/oral_ubiome/nhanes_ubiome_cho/0_manuscript_phyloseq_ubiome/2_figures_out/16.2_oral_d_indices_combined.pdf",
  "/Users/byeongyeoncho/Desktop/oral_ubiome/nhanes_ubiome_cho/0_manuscript_phyloseq_ubiome/2_figures_out/16.2_oral_d_indices_otu_combined.pdf",
  "/Users/byeongyeoncho/Desktop/oral_ubiome/nhanes_ubiome_cho/0_manuscript_phyloseq_ubiome/2_figures_out/16.3_phewas_indices_combined.pdf",
  "/Users/byeongyeoncho/Desktop/oral_ubiome/nhanes_ubiome_cho/0_manuscript_phyloseq_ubiome/2_figures_out/16.3_phewas_indices_otu_combined.pdf",
  "/Users/byeongyeoncho/Desktop/oral_ubiome/nhanes_ubiome_cho/0_manuscript_phyloseq_ubiome/2_figures_out/16.4_exwas_indices_combined.pdf",
  "/Users/byeongyeoncho/Desktop/oral_ubiome/nhanes_ubiome_cho/0_manuscript_phyloseq_ubiome/2_figures_out/16.4_exwas_indices_otu_combined.pdf",
  "/Users/byeongyeoncho/Desktop/oral_ubiome/nhanes_ubiome_cho/0_manuscript_phyloseq_ubiome/2_figures_out/16.5_outwas_indices_combined.pdf",
  "/Users/byeongyeoncho/Desktop/oral_ubiome/nhanes_ubiome_cho/0_manuscript_phyloseq_ubiome/2_figures_out/16.5_outwas_indices_otu_combined.pdf"
)

# Use pdf_pagesize() instead of pdf_info
get_pdf_dimensions <- function(path) {
  if (!file.exists(path)) {
    warning(paste("File does not exist:", path))
    return(NULL)
  }
  dims <- tryCatch(pdf_pagesize(path), error = function(e) {
    warning(paste("Cannot read PDF size:", path, "| Error:", e$message))
    return(NULL)
  })
  if (is.null(dims)) return(NULL)

  width_in <- dims$width[1] / 72
  height_in <- dims$height[1] / 72
  width_mm <- width_in * 25.4
  height_mm <- height_in * 25.4

  data.table(
    file = basename(path),
    width_in = round(width_in, 2),
    height_in = round(height_in, 2),
    width_mm = round(width_mm, 1),
    height_mm = round(height_mm, 1)
  )
}

# Run it
dimensions_dt <- rbindlist(lapply(pdf_paths, get_pdf_dimensions), fill = TRUE)
print(dimensions_dt)

```



signif(mean_abundance, digits = 3)),

sprintf("%.3f", mean_abundance)),


uBiome PheWAS
- Clinical Pheno ~ genus_i + age + sex + poverty + education + race

uBiome ExWas
- genus_i ~ Exposure + age + sex + poverty + education + race + DR1TKCAL_adj + DR2TKCAL_adj + URXUCR_adjSMQ_current_ever_never + 

uBiome Oral Disease
- [Oral Disease] ~ genus_i + age + sex + poverty + education + race

```{R}
##################################################
# 0) Libraries and Helper Functions
##################################################
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(rlang)
library(egg)      # for theme_article
library(ggrepel)  # for text annotation
library(stringr)  # for str_remove_all
library(viridis)  # For Viridis palette
library(readr)    # For read_csv

# Define viz_out_path
viz_out_path <- "/Users/byeongyeoncho/Desktop/oral_ubiome/nhanes_ubiome_cho/0_manuscript_phyloseq_ubiome/2_figures_out"

# Save PDF function
save_pdf_figure <- function(file_name, width_mm, height_mm, plot) {
  pdf(
    file = file.path(viz_out_path, file_name),
    width = width_mm / 25.4, 
    height = height_mm / 25.4,
    family = "Helvetica", colormodel = "rgb",
    pointsize = 6, useDingbats = FALSE
  )
  print(plot)
  dev.off()
}

# Convert p-value to dot size
calc_dot_size <- function(p) {
  case_when(
    p < 1e-6  ~ 2.5,
    p < 1e-4  ~ 1.5,
    p < 1e-2  ~ 0.5,
    TRUE      ~ 0.01
  )
}

# Manually selected 12 distinct colorblind-friendly palette hex codes
otu_colors <- c(
  "#000000", "#E69F00", "#56B4E9", "#009E73", "#F0E442",
  "blue", "darkorange", "#CC79A7", "#999999", "darkgreen",
  "#882255", "darkred"
) 

##################################################
# 1) Unified Plotting Function
##################################################
plot_and_save <- function(df, plot_type = "otu", file_prefix, width_mm, height_mm, 
                          genus_mapping, ubiome_variable_mapping, phylum_df, phylum_colors, 
                          fill_var = NULL, row_var = NULL, otu_col = "otu", estimate_col = "estimate", 
                          fdr_col = "p.value.fdr", split_by_prefix = FALSE, prefix_subsets = NULL, 
                          prefixes_key = NULL, remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, 
                          annotate_na = FALSE, xlim = NULL, show_legend = TRUE, min_annotations = 4) {
  
  # Determine row_var or fill_var if not provided
  if (is.null(fill_var) && plot_type == "otu") {
    fill_var <- intersect(c("phenotype", "exposure"), names(df))[1]
  }
  if (is.null(row_var) && plot_type == "exposure") {
    row_var <- intersect(c("phenotype", "exposure"), names(df))[1]
  }
  
  # Filter by prefix_subsets if provided
  if (!is.null(prefix_subsets) && !is.na(row_var)) {
    df <- df %>% filter(substr(.data[[row_var]], 1, 3) %in% prefix_subsets)
  }
  
  # Base data preparation
  df2 <- df %>%
    distinct(.data[[otu_col]], .data[[estimate_col]], .keep_all = TRUE) %>%
    left_join(genus_mapping %>% distinct(otu, .keep_all = TRUE), by = "otu") %>%
    mutate(dot_size = calc_dot_size(.data[[fdr_col]])) %>%
    filter(!is.na(dot_size))
  
  if (remove_na_unclassified) {
    df2 <- df2 %>% filter(Genus != "unclassified" & !is.na(Genus))
  }
  
  if (nrow(df2) == 0) {
    warning(paste("No significant results for", plot_type, "plot:", file_prefix))
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # OTU Plot
  if (plot_type == "otu") {
    df2 <- df2 %>%
      mutate(
        row_label = if_else(Genus == "unclassified" | is.na(Genus), paste0("NA; ", .data$otu), Genus),
        prefix = substr(.data[[fill_var]], 1, 3)
      )
    
    dummy_row <- tibble(
      otu = "DUMMY_OTU",
      estimate = 0,
      p.value.fdr = 1,
      Genus = "{DUMMY_empty_row_for_padding_placeholder_DUMMY}",
      row_label = "{DUMMY_empty_row_for_padding_placeholder_DUMMY}",
      dot_size = calc_dot_size(1),
      prefix = "DUM"
    )
    df2 <- bind_rows(df2, dummy_row)
    
    assoc_counts <- df2 %>%
      group_by(row_label) %>%
      summarise(Total_Assoc = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
      arrange(Total_Assoc, desc(row_label))  # Sort by Total_Assoc, then reverse alphabetical for ties
    
    df2$row_label <- factor(df2$row_label, levels = assoc_counts$row_label)
    
    p <- ggplot(df2, aes(x = estimate, y = row_label, fill = prefix, size = dot_size)) +
      geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black", size = 0.2) +
      geom_point(shape = 21, alpha = 0.5, stroke = 0.2) +
      scale_size_identity(guide = if (show_legend) "legend" else "none", 
                          breaks = c(2.5, 1.5, 0.5), 
                          labels = c("< 1e-6", "1e-6 to 1e-4", "1e-4 to 1e-2"),
                          name = "FDR p-value") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = otu_colors, name = "Prefix") +
      theme_article() +
      theme(
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 6),
        legend.position = if (show_legend) "bottom" else "none",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 6),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 6)
      ) +
      labs(x = "Estimate", y = "Genus (OTU)", fill = "Prefix")
    
    if (!is.null(xlim)) p <- p + xlim(xlim[1], xlim[2])
  }
  
  # Exposure Plot
  if (plot_type == "exposure") {
    df2 <- df2 %>%
      left_join(phylum_df %>% distinct(otu, .keep_all = TRUE), by = setNames("otu", otu_col)) %>%
      left_join(ubiome_variable_mapping %>% select(var_name, var_description), by = setNames("var_name", row_var)) %>%
      mutate(
        prefix = if (split_by_prefix) substr(.data[[row_var]], 1, 3) else "All",
        row_label = if_else(is.na(var_description), .data[[row_var]], var_description)
      )
    
    if (split_by_prefix) {
      unique_prefixes <- unique(df2$prefix)
      dummy_rows <- tibble(
        otu = "DUMMY_OTU",
        estimate = 0,
        p.value.fdr = 1,
        Phylum = "unclassified",
        Genus = "{DUMMY_empty_row_for_padding_placeholder_DUMMY}",
        row_label = "{DUMMY_empty_row_for_padding_placeholder_DUMMY}",
        dot_size = calc_dot_size(1),
        prefix = unique_prefixes
      ) %>% mutate(!!row_var := "{DUMMY_empty_row_for_padding_placeholder_DUMMY}")
    } else {
      dummy_rows <- tibble(
        otu = "DUMMY_OTU",
        estimate = 0,
        p.value.fdr = 1,
        Phylum = "unclassified",
        Genus = "{DUMMY_empty_row_for_padding_placeholder_DUMMY}",
        row_label = "{DUMMY_empty_row_for_padding_placeholder_DUMMY}",
        dot_size = calc_dot_size(1),
        prefix = "All"
      ) %>% mutate(!!row_var := "{DUMMY_empty_row_for_padding_placeholder_DUMMY}")
    }
    df2 <- bind_rows(df2, dummy_rows)
    
    # Corrected ordering
    if (split_by_prefix) {
      assoc_counts <- df2 %>%
        group_by(prefix, row_label) %>%
        summarise(Total_Assoc = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
        arrange(prefix, Total_Assoc, desc(row_label))  # Sort by prefix, Total_Assoc, then reverse alphabetical for ties
      
      # Create unique levels per prefix using a grouped approach
      df2 <- df2 %>%
        group_by(prefix) %>%
        mutate(row_label = factor(row_label, levels = assoc_counts$row_label[assoc_counts$prefix == unique(prefix)])) %>%
        ungroup()
    } else {
      assoc_counts <- df2 %>%
        group_by(row_label) %>%
        summarise(Total_Assoc = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
        arrange(Total_Assoc, desc(row_label))  # Sort by Total_Assoc, then reverse alphabetical for ties
      
      df2$row_label <- factor(df2$row_label, levels = assoc_counts$row_label)
    }
    
    df_annotate <- if (split_by_prefix) {
      df2 %>%
        group_by(prefix, row_label) %>%
        mutate(abs_estimate = abs(.data[[estimate_col]])) %>%
        arrange(desc(abs_estimate), .by_group = TRUE) %>%
        slice(seq_len(max(min_annotations, ceiling(n() * 0.2)))) %>%
        ungroup() %>%
        mutate(label = if_else(Genus == "unclassified" | is.na(Genus), "NA", Genus)) %>%
        distinct(prefix, row_label, .data[[estimate_col]], .keep_all = TRUE)
    } else {
      df2 %>%
        group_by(row_label) %>%
        mutate(abs_estimate = abs(.data[[estimate_col]])) %>%
        arrange(desc(abs_estimate), .by_group = TRUE) %>%
        slice(seq_len(max(min_annotations, ceiling(n() * 0.2)))) %>%
        ungroup() %>%
        mutate(label = if_else(Genus == "unclassified" | is.na(Genus), "NA", Genus)) %>%
        distinct(row_label, .data[[estimate_col]], .keep_all = TRUE)
    }
    
    if (!annotate_na) {
      df_annotate <- df_annotate %>% filter(Genus != "unclassified" & !is.na(Genus))
    }
    
    p <- ggplot(df2, aes(x = .data[[estimate_col]], y = row_label, fill = Phylum, size = dot_size)) +
      geom_vline(xintercept = 0, color = "black", size = 0.2) +
      geom_point(shape = 21, alpha = 0.5, stroke = 0.15) +
      geom_text_repel(data = df_annotate, aes(label = label), size = 5/2.83465, 
                      box.padding = 0.9, point.padding = 0.1, segment.color = "gray50", segment.size = 0.3) +
      scale_size_identity(guide = if (show_legend) "legend" else "none", 
                          breaks = c(2.5, 1.5, 0.5), 
                          labels = c("< 1e-6", "1e-6 to 1e-4", "1e-4 to 1e-2"),
                          name = "FDR p-value") +
      scale_fill_manual(values = phylum_colors, na.value = "grey90") +
      theme_article() +
      theme(
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 6),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6),
        axis.title = element_text(size = 6),
        legend.position = if (show_legend) "bottom" else "none",
        legend.text = element_text(size = 6),
        legend.title = element_text(size = 6)
      ) +
      labs(x = "Estimate", y = "Phenotype/Exposure Description", fill = "Phylum")
    
    if (split_by_prefix) p <- p + facet_wrap(~prefix, scales = "free_y")
    if (!is.null(xlim)) p <- p + xlim(xlim[1], xlim[2])
  }
  
  # Save the plot
  file_name <- paste0(file_prefix, ".pdf")
  save_pdf_figure(file_name, width_mm = width_mm, height_mm = height_mm, plot = p)
  
  return(p)
}

##################################################
# 3) Color mappings & phylum info
##################################################
phylum_colors <- c(
  "Firmicutes"           = "#E69F00",
  "Bacteroidetes"        = "#56B4E9",
  "Actinobacteria"       = "#009E73",
  "Proteobacteria"       = "#F0E442",
  "Fusobacteria"         = "#CC6677",
  "Spirochaetae"         = "#D55E00",
  "Cyanobacteria"        = "#CC79A7",
  "Acidobacteria"        = "#999999",
  "Candidate division SR1" = "#AD7700",
  "Planctomycetes"       = "#332288",
  "Saccharibacteria"     = "#44AA99",
  "Synergistetes"        = "#88CCEE",
  "Tenericutes"          = "#117733",
  "unclassified"         = "#DDDDDD",
  "Verrucomicrobia"      = "#0072B2"
)

phylum_info <- data.frame(
  otu    = rownames(tax_table(ubiome_relative)),
  Phylum = as.character(tax_table(ubiome_relative)[, "Phylum"]),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

genus_mapping <- data.frame(
  otu   = rownames(tax_table(ubiome_relative)),
  Genus = tax_table(ubiome_relative)[, "Genus"],
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

ubiome_variable_mapping <- read_csv(
  "/Users/byeongyeoncho/Desktop/oral_ubiome/nhanes_ubiome_cho/0_manuscript_phyloseq_ubiome/3_supplementary_out/ubiome_variable_mapping.csv",
  locale = locale(encoding = "UTF-8")
)

##################################################
# 4) Generate & Save Plots
##################################################
datasets <- list(
  phewas  = phewas_sig_res,
  exwas   = exwas_sig_res,
  demowas = demowas_sig_res,
  oral_d  = oral_d_sig_res,
  outwas  = outwas_sig_res
)

plot_configs <- list(
  # OTU Plots - USE remove_na_unclassified = TRUE

### **F1 – DEMOWAS**

# list(df = datasets$demowas, plot_type = "otu", file_prefix = "F1_sup_demowas_otu_plot", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 175, remove_na = TRUE, remove_na_unclassified = TRUE),
# list(df = datasets$demowas, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "F1_full_demowas_exp_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 70, remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2),
# list(df = datasets$demowas, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "F2_demowas_exp_AGE_RID_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 35, prefix_subsets = c("AGE", "RID"), prefixes_key = "AGE_RID", remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2),
# 
# ### **F3 – ORAL_D**
# 
# list(df = datasets$oral_d, plot_type = "otu", file_prefix = "F3_sup_oral_d_otu_plot", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 125, remove_na = TRUE, remove_na_unclassified = TRUE),
# list(df = datasets$oral_d, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "F3_full_oral_d_exp_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 40, remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2),
# list(df = datasets$oral_d, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "F3_oral_d_exp_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 40, remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2),
#  
list(df = datasets$exwas, plot_type = "otu", file_prefix = "F4_sup_exwas_otu_plot", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 185, remove_na = TRUE, remove_na_unclassified = TRUE),
list(df = datasets$exwas, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "F4_full_exwas_exp_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 230, remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2), 
list(df = datasets$exwas, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "F4_exwas_exp_DR1_DR2_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 100, prefix_subsets = c("DR1", "DR2"), prefixes_key = "DR1_DR2", remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2),
list(df = datasets$exwas, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "F4_exwas_exp_DS1_DS2_SMQ_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 40, prefix_subsets = c("DS1", "DS2", "SMQ"), prefixes_key = "DS1_DS2_SMQ", remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2),
list(df = datasets$exwas, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "F4_exwas_exp_LBX_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 120, prefix_subsets = c("LBX"), prefixes_key = "LBX", remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2),
 
list(df = datasets$phewas, plot_type = "otu", file_prefix = "F5_sup_phewas_otu_plot", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 225, remove_na = TRUE, remove_na_unclassified = TRUE),
list(df = datasets$phewas, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "F5_full_phewas_exp_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 235, remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2),
list(df = datasets$phewas, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "F5_phewas_exp_LBX_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 220, prefix_subsets = c("LBX"), prefixes_key = "LBX", remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2),
list(df = datasets$phewas, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "F5_phewas_exp_LBD_URX_ENX_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 75, prefix_subsets = c("LBD", "URX", "ENX"), prefixes_key = "LBD_URX_ENX", remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2),
list(df = datasets$phewas, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "F5_phewas_exp_BMX_BPX_MDI_MSY_SPX_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 75, prefix_subsets = c("BMX", "BPX", "MDI", "MSY", "SPX"), prefixes_key = "BMX_BPX_MDI_MSY_SPX", remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2),

### **F6 – OUTWAS**
list(df = datasets$outwas, plot_type = "otu", file_prefix = "F6_outwas_otu_plot", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 145, remove_na = TRUE, remove_na_unclassified = TRUE),
list(df = datasets$outwas, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "F6_outwas_exp_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 87, remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2),
list(df = datasets$outwas, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "F6_ outwas_exp_CAN_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 38, prefix_subsets = c("CAN"), prefixes_key = "CAN", remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2),
list(df = datasets$outwas, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "F6_outwas_exp_CHD_HEA_ANG_STR_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 42, prefix_subsets = c("CHD", "HEA", "ANG", "STR"), prefixes_key = "CHD_HEA_ANG_STR", remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2, xlim = c(-100, 0)),
list(df = datasets$outwas, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "F6_outwas_exp_EMP_AST_BRO_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 38, prefix_subsets = c("EMP", "AST", "BRO"), prefixes_key = "EMP_AST_BRO", remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2, xlim = c(-100, 0)),
list(df = datasets$outwas, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "F6_outwas_exp_DIA_CVD_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 30, prefix_subsets = c("DIA", "CVD"), prefixes_key = "DIA_CVD", remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2),

# F6 – OUTWAS (Version with xlim = c(-15, 15))
list(df = datasets$outwas, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "F6_1_outwas_exp_CAN_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 38, prefix_subsets = c("CAN"), prefixes_key = "CAN", remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2, xlim = c(-15, 5)),
list(df = datasets$outwas, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "F6_1_outwas_exp_CHD_HEA_ANG_STR_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 42, prefix_subsets = c("CHD", "HEA", "ANG", "STR"), prefixes_key = "CHD_HEA_ANG_STR", remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2, xlim = c(-15, 5)),
list(df = datasets$outwas, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "F6_1_outwas_exp_EMP_AST_BRO_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 38, prefix_subsets = c("EMP", "AST", "BRO"), prefixes_key = "EMP_AST_BRO", remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2, xlim = c(-15, 5)),
list(df = datasets$outwas, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "F6_1_outwas_exp_DIA_CVD_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 115, height_mm = 30, prefix_subsets = c("DIA", "CVD"), prefixes_key = "DIA_CVD", remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2, xlim = c(-1.5, 0.5))


  # Exposure Plots (Split by Prefix) - USE remove_na_unclassified = FALSE

  # list(df = datasets$phewas, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "12.3_phewas_exp_split_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 240, height_mm = 180, split_by_prefix = TRUE, remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2),
  # 
  # list(df = datasets$exwas, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "12.3_exwas_exp_split_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 240, height_mm = 180, split_by_prefix = TRUE, remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2),
  # 
  # list(df = datasets$demowas, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "12.3_demowas_exp_split_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 240, height_mm = 180, split_by_prefix = TRUE, remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2),
  # 
  # list(df = datasets$oral_d, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "12.3_oral_d_exp_split_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 240, height_mm = 180, split_by_prefix = TRUE, remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2),
  # 
  # list(df = datasets$outwas, plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "12.3_outwas_exp_split_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 240, height_mm = 180, split_by_prefix = TRUE, remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2),
  
#   # Special Demowas Subset
#   list(df = datasets$demowas %>% filter(exposure %in% c("AGE_SQUARED", "RIDAGEYR")), plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "12.5_demowas_exp_subset_plot", width_mm = 80, height_mm = 40, remove_na = TRUE, remove_na_unclassified = TRUE, min_annotations = 2),
#   list(df = datasets$demowas %>% filter(exposure %in% c("AGE_SQUARED", "RIDAGEYR")), plot_type = "exposure", file_prefix = "12.5_demowas_exp_subset_plot_incl_NA", width_mm = 80, height_mm = 40, remove_na = FALSE, remove_na_unclassified = FALSE, min_annotations = 2)
)

# Generate all plots with one function call per PDF
for (config in plot_configs) {
  plot_and_save(
    df = config$df,
    plot_type = config$plot_type,
    file_prefix = config$file_prefix,
    width_mm = config$width_mm,
    height_mm = config$height_mm,
    genus_mapping = genus_mapping,
    ubiome_variable_mapping = ubiome_variable_mapping,
    phylum_df = phylum_info,
    phylum_colors = phylum_colors,
    split_by_prefix = config$split_by_prefix %||% FALSE,
    prefix_subsets = config$prefix_subsets,
    prefixes_key = config$prefixes_key,
    remove_na = config$remove_na %||% FALSE,
    remove_na_unclassified = config$remove_na_unclassified %||% FALSE,
    annotate_na = FALSE,
    xlim = config$xlim,
    show_legend = TRUE,
    min_annotations = config$min_annotations %||% 4
  )
}
```


```{r}
# Load necessary libraries
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(phyloseq)
library(gridExtra)

# Assuming these are loaded:
# - ubiome_relative (phyloseq object)
# - outwas_sample_data (RDS with SEQN and phenotypes)
# - intermediate_files_path (for loading RDS)

# Load outwas_sample_data if not already loaded
outwas_sample_data <- readRDS(file.path(intermediate_files_path, "sample_data_outwas.rds"))

# Extract OTU table with OTUs as columns and samples as rows
otu_table_raw <- as.data.frame(otu_table(ubiome_relative), stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
if (taxa_are_rows(ubiome_relative)) {
  otu_table_raw <- t(otu_table_raw)  # Transpose if taxa are rows to get OTUs as columns
}
otu_table_raw <- otu_table_raw %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  tibble::rownames_to_column("SEQN")  # Sample IDs as SEQN column

# Merge with outwas_sample_data to get phenotype data
combined_data <- otu_table_raw %>%
  left_join(outwas_sample_data, by = "SEQN")

# Verify columns match expected OTUs
print("Sample of OTU columns in combined_data:")
print(head(colnames(combined_data)[2:7]))  # Skip SEQN, show first few OTU columns

# Function to create diagnostic plots for a given phenotype and OTU
create_diagnostic_plots <- function(phenotype, otu, data, top_n = 5) {
  # Check if OTU exists in data
  if (!otu %in% colnames(data)) {
    warning(paste("OTU", otu, "not found in combined_data"))
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Subset data for the specific OTU and phenotype
  plot_data <- data %>%
    select(SEQN, all_of(otu), all_of(phenotype)) %>%
    rename(abundance = !!otu, status = !!phenotype) %>%
    filter(!is.na(status)) %>%
    mutate(status = factor(status, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Control", "Case")))

  # Boxplot: Abundance by case/control status
  box_plot <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = status, y = abundance, fill = status)) +
    geom_boxplot(outlier.size = 0.5) +
    labs(title = paste("Boxplot:", otu, "vs", phenotype),
         x = "Status", y = "Raw Abundance",
         fill = "Group") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom")

  # Histogram: Underlying distribution of abundance
  hist_plot <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = abundance)) +
    geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "grey70", color = "black") +
    labs(title = paste("Histogram:", otu, "Abundance"),
         x = "Raw Abundance", y = "Count") +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 10)

  return(list(box = box_plot, hist = hist_plot))
}

# Select top OTUs for CANCER_LUNG and CANCER_ESOPHAGEAL
top_lung <- outwas_sig_res %>%
  filter(phenotype == "CANCER_COLON") %>%
  arrange(-abs(estimate)) %>%
  head(5) %>%
  pull(otu)

top_esoph <- outwas_sig_res %>%
  filter(phenotype == "CANCER_ESOPHAGEAL") %>%
  arrange(-abs(estimate)) %>%
  head(5) %>%
  pull(otu)

# Generate plots for top OTUs
lung_plots <- lapply(top_lung, function(otu) {
  create_diagnostic_plots("CANCER_PROSTATE", otu, combined_data)
})
lung_plots <- lung_plots[!sapply(lung_plots, is.null)]  # Remove NULL plots

esoph_plots <- lapply(top_esoph, function(otu) {
  create_diagnostic_plots("CANCER_PROSTATE", otu, combined_data)
})
esoph_plots <- esoph_plots[!sapply(esoph_plots, is.null)]  # Remove NULL plots

# Save plots to PDF
save_pdf_figure("14.1_Diagnostic_Plots_CANCER_LUNG.pdf", width_mm = 200, height_mm = 250)
do.call(grid.arrange, c(lapply(lung_plots, function(x) x$box), list(ncol = 2)))
dev.off()

save_pdf_figure("14.2_Diagnostic_Plots_CANCER_LUNG_Distribution.pdf", width_mm = 200, height_mm = 250)
do.call(grid.arrange, c(lapply(lung_plots, function(x) x$hist), list(ncol = 2)))
dev.off()

save_pdf_figure("14.3_Diagnostic_Plots_CANCER_ESOPHAGEAL.pdf", width_mm = 200, height_mm = 250)
do.call(grid.arrange, c(lapply(esoph_plots, function(x) x$box), list(ncol = 2)))
dev.off()

save_pdf_figure("14.4_Diagnostic_Plots_CANCER_ESOPHAGEAL_Distribution.pdf", width_mm = 200, height_mm = 250)
do.call(grid.arrange, c(lapply(esoph_plots, function(x) x$hist), list(ncol = 2)))
dev.off()
```

### **Plot Description Summary**

This code generates two types of plots from regression results linking OTU abundances to exposures or phenotypes, visualizing effect sizes and significance.

---

## **1. OTU-Level Plot (Estimate vs OTU, Genus-Labeled)**
### **Axes and Aesthetics:**
- **Y-axis:** Genus name of OTU (or “NA; OTU_ID” if unclassified).
- **X-axis:** Estimate (regression coefficient).
- **Fill:** Exposure or phenotype associated with the OTU.
- **Dot Size:** -log10(FDR), representing statistical significance.

### **Data Processing:**
- **OTU names are replaced with Genus names** using `genus_mapping` (NA if unclassified).
- **Rows are filtered:** Only significant associations (`p.value.fdr` not NA) are plotted.
- **Rows are ordered:** **Most significant OTUs (lowest FDR)** appear at the **top**.

### **Interpretation:**
- **Top rows = Stronger statistical associations (lower FDR).**
- **Larger dots = Higher significance (lower FDR).**
- **X-axis position = Effect size (positive or negative association).**

---

## **2. Exposure-Level Plot (Estimate vs Exposure/Phenotype, Phylum Fill)**
### **Axes and Aesthetics:**
- **Y-axis:** Exposure or phenotype.
- **X-axis:** Estimate (regression coefficient).
- **Fill:** OTU phylum.
- **Dot Size:** -log10(FDR), representing statistical significance.

### **Data Processing:**
- **OTU-phylum mapping** via `phylum_info` (NA phylum = grey fill).
- **Rows are filtered:** Only significant associations (`p.value.fdr` not NA) are plotted.
- **Rows are ordered:** **Most significant exposures/phenotypes (lowest FDR)** appear at the **top**.

### **Interpretation:**
- **Top rows = Exposures/phenotypes with stronger OTU associations (lower FDR).**
- **Larger dots = Higher significance (lower FDR).**
- **X-axis position = Effect size (positive or negative association).**

---

## **Key Filtering and Ordering Logic (Both Plots):**
| **Step**                        | **Effect**                                             |
|----------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| **Filter:** `p.value.fdr != NA` | Only significant associations are plotted.            |
| **Order:** Ascending `p.value.fdr` | Most significant associations appear at the **top**. |
| **Dot Size:** -log10(FDR)       | Larger dots indicate more significant associations.   |





## Comprehensive Description of pre-loaded Data Content, Structure, and Mapping with `ubiome_relative` (phyloseq) Object

This document provides an overview of the structure and content of each loaded data object. It also explains their mapping relationships with the `ubiome_relative` phyloseq object, serving as a guide for researchers working with these datasets.

---

### 1. Sample Data Objects
#### a. `phenotype_sample_data`
- **Type:** tibble [9847 x 77]
- **Description:** Contains anthropometric and clinical measurements for each subject.
- **Key Variables:**
  - `SEQN` (Primary Key): Unique subject identifier.
  - Anthropometric variables: `BMXBMI` (Body Mass Index), `BMXWAIST` (Waist Circumference), etc.
  - Biochemical markers: `LBDHDD` (HDL cholesterol), `LBXGLU` (Glucose), etc.
- **Mapping:** Maps to `ubiome_relative@sam_data$SEQN` through `SEQN`.

#### b. `exposure_sample_data`
- **Type:** tibble [9847 x 330]
- **Description:** Dietary, physical activity, and environmental exposure data.
- **Key Variables:**
  - `SEQN` (Primary Key)
  - Dietary intake: `DR1TPROT` (Protein intake), `DR1TVC` (Vitamin C intake), etc.
  - Chemical exposure: `LBXEPAH` (Polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons), etc.
- **Mapping:** Maps to `ubiome_relative@sam_data$SEQN` through `SEQN`.

#### c. `oral_disease_sample_data`
- **Type:** tibble [9847 x 5]
- **Description:** Self-reported oral health conditions.
- **Key Variables:**
  - `SEQN` (Primary Key)
  - `TOOTH_DECAY_OHAROCDT` (Tooth Decay), `GUM_DISEASE_OHAROCGP` (Gum Disease), etc.
- **Mapping:** Maps to `ubiome_relative@sam_data$SEQN` through `SEQN`.

#### d. `demo_sample_data`
- **Type:** tibble [9847 x 13]
- **Description:** Demographic information.
- **Key Variables:**
  - `SEQN` (Primary Key)
  - `RIDAGEYR` (Age), `INDFMPIR` (Poverty-income ratio), `ETHNICITY_MEXICAN`, etc.
- **Mapping:** Maps to `ubiome_relative@sam_data$SEQN` through `SEQN`.



### 2. Regression Results Objects (Tidy Outputs)
#### a. `demowas_tidied`
- **Type:** tibble [7124 x 15]
- **Description:** Tidy results from demographic association tests with genus-level relative abundance.
- **Key Variables:**
  - `exposure`: Demographic variable (e.g., `RIDAGEYR`).
  - `otu`: OTU identifier (e.g., `RSV_genus10_relative`).
  - `estimate`, `p.value`, `p.value.fdr`: Regression coefficient and significance.
- **Mapping:** Links demographic variables from `demo_sample_data` with genus-level OTUs from `ubiome_relative@otu_table`.

#### b. `exwas_tidied`
- **Type:** tibble [1165104 x 15]
- **Description:** Tidy results from exposure association tests.
- **Key Variables:**
  - `exposure`: Exposure variable (e.g., `DR1TACAR`).
  - `otu`: OTU identifier.
- **Mapping:** Links exposure variables from `exposure_sample_data` with genus-level OTUs from `ubiome_relative@otu_table`.

#### c. `phewas_tidied`
- **Type:** tibble [221908 x 15]
- **Description:** Tidy results from phenotype association tests.
- **Key Variables:**
  - `phenotype`: Phenotype variable (e.g., `BMXBMI`).
  - `otu`: OTU identifier.
- **Mapping:** Links phenotype variables from `phenotype_sample_data` with genus-level OTUs from `ubiome_relative@otu_table`.

#### d. `oral_d_tidied`
- **Type:** tibble [4613 x 13]
- **Description:** Tidy results from oral disease association tests.
- **Key Variables:**
  - `phenotype`: Oral disease variable (e.g., `TOOTH_DECAY_OHAROCDT`).
  - `otu`: OTU identifier.
- **Mapping:** Links oral disease variables from `oral_disease_sample_data` with genus-level OTUs from `ubiome_relative@otu_table`.

---

### 3. Model Summary Objects (Glanced Outputs)
#### a. `demowas_glanced`
#### b. `exwas_glanced`
#### c. `phewas_glanced`
#### d. `oral_d_glanced`
- **Type:** tibbles containing model-level statistics for each exposure-OTU association test.
- **Key Variables:**
  - `model_number`, `AIC`, `BIC`, `adj.r2`, `deviance`, `df.null`, `df.residual`.
- **Mapping:** Summary statistics corresponding to `*_tidied` results.

---

### 4. Model Fit Quality Objects (R-squared Outputs)
#### a. `demowas_rsq`
#### b. `exwas_rsq`
#### c. `phewas_rsq`
#### d. `oral_d_rsq`
- **Type:** tibbles with R-squared and adjusted R-squared values for each model.
- **Key Variables:**
  - `rsq`, `adj.r2`, `model_number`.
- **Mapping:** Fit quality measures corresponding to `*_tidied` and `*_glanced` results.

---

### 5. Microbiome Data Object
#### `ubiome_relative`
- **Type:** phyloseq object with:
  - `otu_table`: Abundance matrix (1349 OTUs x 9847 samples).
  - `tax_table`: Taxonomic assignments (Domain to Genus).
  - `sam_data`: Sample metadata (e.g., demographics, clinical data).
  - `phy_tree`: Phylogenetic tree of OTUs.
- **Mapping:**
  - `otu_table`: `RSV_genus*_relative` identifiers align with `otu` in `*_tidied` objects.
  - `sam_data$SEQN`: Links with `SEQN` in sample data objects.

---

### 6.Genus Mapping
# genus_mapping <- data.frame(
#   otu   = rownames(tax_table(ubiome_relative)),
#   Genus = tax_table(ubiome_relative)[, "Genus"],
#   stringsAsFactors = FALSE
# )
Map OTU row identifiers to their genuses using genus_mapping, ensuring "unclassified" are named "NA; otuName", and updating row names of final_mat.

### Summary of Key Relationships
| Data Object               | Key Identifier | Mapping Target (ubiome_relative)  |
|---------------------------|----------------|------------------------------------|
| `phenotype_sample_data`    | `SEQN`         | `sam_data$SEQN`                   |
| `exposure_sample_data`     | `SEQN`         | `sam_data$SEQN`                   |
| `oral_disease_sample_data` | `SEQN`         | `sam_data$SEQN`                   |
| `demo_sample_data`         | `SEQN`         | `sam_data$SEQN`                   |
| `*_tidied`                 | `otu`          | `otu_table`                       |
| `*_glanced`                | `model_number` | `*_tidied$model_number`           |
| `*_rsq`                    | `model_number` | `*_tidied$model_number`           |

Researchers should integrate these data objects by leveraging the shared identifiers (`SEQN`, `otu`, `model_number`). The `ubiome_relative` phyloseq object serves as the core microbial abundance and taxonomy reference.


```{r}
#==================================================
# Minimal, Efficient Code for Group-Comparison Log2-Mean Heatmaps
# with Genus Mapping for Row Labels
#==================================================

# ---- Libraries ----
library(phyloseq)      # (https://doi.org/10.18129/B9.bioc.phyloseq)
library(dplyr)         # (https://doi.org/10.18637/jss.v084.i08)
library(ComplexHeatmap)# (https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/btw313)
library(circlize)     
library(grid)          

# ---- Assuming these are already loaded: ----
#  1) ubiome_relative  (phyloseq)
#  2) demowas_tidied, exwas_tidied, phewas_tidied, oral_d_tidied, outwas_sig_res (tibbles)
#  3) save_pdf_figure()   # see your code above
#  4) genus_mapping (data.frame with columns: `otu`, `Genus`)
#  5) viz_out_path, intermediate_files_path, outwas_sample_data (RDS from earlier)

#==================================================
# A) Subset Function
#==================================================
subset_phyloseq_by_significant_otus <- function(phy, sig_tibble, col_otu = "otu") {
  otus <- unique(sig_tibble[[col_otu]])
  prune_taxa(taxa_names(phy) %in% otus, phy)
}

#==================================================
# B) Helper for converting sample-data columns to grouped factors
#==================================================
unify_variable_type <- function(x, n_quintiles = 5) {
  x_non_na <- x[!is.na(x)]
  distinct_vals <- unique(x_non_na)
  if (length(distinct_vals) < 2) return(NULL)

  if (is.numeric(x)) {
    if (length(distinct_vals) == 2) {
      lvls <- sort(distinct_vals)
      return(factor(x, levels = lvls))
    } else {
      cuts <- quantile(x, probs = seq(0, 1, length.out = n_quintiles + 1), na.rm = TRUE)
      cuts <- unique(cuts)
      if (length(cuts) < 2) return(NULL)
      return(cut(x, breaks = cuts, include.lowest = TRUE,
                 labels = paste0("Q", seq_len(length(cuts) - 1))))
    }
  } else {
    f <- factor(as.character(x))
    if (nlevels(f) < 2) return(NULL)
    return(f)
  }
}

#==================================================
# C) Compute row-wise log2(Means) across grouping
#==================================================
compute_group_means_log2matrix <- function(phy, var_name,
                                           top_n       = 20,
                                           epsilon     = 1e-6,
                                           genus_mapping = NULL) {
  sd <- as.data.frame(sample_data(phy))
  if (!var_name %in% colnames(sd)) return(NULL)

  # Convert grouping variable
  grouped <- unify_variable_type(sd[[var_name]])
  if (is.null(grouped)) return(NULL)
  sd[[var_name]] <- grouped
  sample_data(phy) <- phyloseq::sample_data(sd)

  # OTU table
  otu_mat <- as(otu_table(phy), "matrix")
  if (!taxa_are_rows(phy)) {
    otu_mat <- t(otu_mat)
  }

  # Calculate group means
  gr_lvls <- levels(grouped)
  group_means <- sapply(gr_lvls, function(lv) {
    idx <- which(grouped == lv)
    if (length(idx) > 0) Matrix::rowMeans(otu_mat[, idx, drop = FALSE]) else rep(NA, nrow(otu_mat))
  })
  rownames(group_means) <- rownames(otu_mat)

  # log2 transform
  log_mat <- log2(group_means + epsilon)

  # Pick top n by dynamic range
  rng <- apply(log_mat, 1, function(x) max(x, na.rm = TRUE) - min(x, na.rm = TRUE))
  top_idx <- head(order(abs(rng), decreasing = TRUE), top_n)
  final_mat <- log_mat[top_idx, , drop = FALSE]

  # Rename rows using genus_mapping
  if (!is.null(genus_mapping)) {
    row_map <- data.frame(OTU = rownames(final_mat), stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
      left_join(genus_mapping, by = c("OTU" = "otu")) %>%
      mutate(
        Genus = if_else(
          Genus == "unclassified" | is.na(Genus),
          paste0("NA; ", gsub("^RSV_|^OTU_", "", OTU)),
          Genus
        )
      ) %>%
      mutate(Genus = str_remove_all(Genus, "group|_relative"))
  
    rownames(final_mat) <- row_map$Genus
  }
  final_mat
}

#==================================================
# D) Make Single Heatmap (ComplexHeatmap)
#==================================================
make_log2means_heatmap <- function(mat, main_title,
                                   cut_height  = 3,
                                   dist_method = "euclidean",
                                   hc_method   = "complete") {
  if (is.null(mat) || nrow(mat) < 2 || ncol(mat) < 2) return(NULL)

  # Color scale
  val_min <- min(mat, na.rm = TRUE)
  val_max <- max(mat, na.rm = TRUE)
  val_mid <- (val_min + val_max) / 2
  col_fun <- circlize::colorRamp2(c(val_min, val_mid, val_max),
                                  c("darkblue", "white", "red"))

  # Row dendrogram & membership
  d <- dist(mat, method = dist_method)
  hc <- hclust(d, method = hc_method)
  dend <- as.dendrogram(hc)
  grps <- cutree(hc, h = cut_height)
  k <- length(unique(grps))

  Heatmap(
    mat,
    name = "log2(Mean)",
    col  = col_fun,
    cluster_rows    = dend,
    row_split       = k,
    row_dend_reorder= TRUE,
    cluster_columns = TRUE,
    show_row_names  = TRUE,
    show_column_names = TRUE,
    row_names_gp    = gpar(fontsize = 6),
    column_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 6),
    heatmap_legend_param = list(
      title     = "log2(Mean)",
      title_gp  = gpar(fontsize = 6),
      labels_gp = gpar(fontsize = 6)
    ),
    row_dend_width     = unit(0.3, "cm"),
    column_dend_height = unit(0.3, "cm"),
    column_title    = main_title,
    column_title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 6, fontface = "bold")
  )
}

#==================================================
# E) Build a list of heatmaps for multiple variables
#==================================================
build_heatmaps_list <- function(phy, vars, analysis_name,
                                genus_mapping = NULL,
                                cut_height    = 3,
                                dist_method   = "euclidean",
                                hc_method     = "complete") {
  lapply(vars, function(v) {
    mat <- compute_group_means_log2matrix(
      phy, v, top_n = 20, epsilon = 1e-6,
      genus_mapping = genus_mapping
    )
    if (is.null(mat)) return(NULL)
    make_log2means_heatmap(
      mat,
      main_title  = paste0(analysis_name, ": ", v),
      cut_height  = cut_height,
      dist_method = dist_method,
      hc_method   = hc_method
    )
  })
}

#==================================================
# F) Draw multiple heatmaps in 4x4 grids
#==================================================
chunk_list <- function(x, chunk_size = 16) {
  if (length(x) == 0) return(list())
  inds <- seq_along(x)
  split(x, ceiling(inds / chunk_size))
}

draw_heatmaps_in_4x4_grid <- function(ht_list, pdf_file_name,
                                      width_mm  = 275,
                                      height_mm = 310) {
  ht_list <- ht_list[!vapply(ht_list, is.null, logical(1))]
  if (length(ht_list) == 0) return(invisible(NULL))

  save_pdf_figure(pdf_file_name, width_mm, height_mm)
  on.exit(dev.off(), add = TRUE)

  splitted <- chunk_list(ht_list, 16)
  for (chunk in splitted) {
    grid.newpage()
    pushViewport(viewport(layout = grid.layout(4, 4)))
    for (i in seq_along(chunk)) {
      row_i <- ((i - 1) %/% 4) + 1
      col_i <- ((i - 1) %% 4) + 1
      pushViewport(viewport(layout.pos.row = row_i, layout.pos.col = col_i))
      draw(chunk[[i]], newpage = FALSE, merge_legend = TRUE, auto_adjust = FALSE)
      popViewport()
    }
    popViewport()
  }
}

#==================================================
# G) Execute for 5 analyses:
#    1) PheWAS
#    2) ExWAS
#    3) DemoWAS
#    4) OralDisease
#    5) OutWas
#==================================================
# ---- 0) Genus Mapping ----
genus_mapping <- data.frame(
  otu   = rownames(tax_table(ubiome_relative)),
  Genus = tax_table(ubiome_relative)[, "Genus"],
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Load outwas_sample_data to merge with ubiome_relative
outwas_sample_data <- readRDS(file.path(intermediate_files_path, "sample_data_outwas.rds"))

# ---- 1) PheWAS OTU subset ----
phewas_otus  <- unique(phewas_tidied$otu)
ubiome_phewas <- prune_taxa(taxa_names(ubiome_relative) %in% phewas_otus, ubiome_relative)
phewas_vars   <- unique(phewas_tidied$phenotype)

phewas_hts    <- build_heatmaps_list(
  phy            = ubiome_phewas,
  vars           = phewas_vars,
  analysis_name  = "PheWAS",
  genus_mapping  = genus_mapping
)
draw_heatmaps_in_4x4_grid(
  ht_list       = phewas_hts,
  pdf_file_name = "13.2_Log2Mean_GroupComparison_Heatmaps_Phewas.pdf"
)

# ---- 2) ExWAS OTU subset ----
exwas_otus   <- unique(exwas_tidied$otu)
ubiome_exwas <- prune_taxa(taxa_names(ubiome_relative) %in% exwas_otus, ubiome_relative)
exwas_vars   <- unique(exwas_tidied$exposure)

exwas_hts    <- build_heatmaps_list(
  phy            = ubiome_exwas,
  vars           = exwas_vars,
  analysis_name  = "ExWAS",
  genus_mapping  = genus_mapping
)
draw_heatmaps_in_4x4_grid(
  ht_list       = exwas_hts,
  pdf_file_name = "13.3_Log2Mean_GroupComparison_Heatmaps_Exwas.pdf"
)

# ---- 3) DemoWAS OTU subset ----
demowas_otus   <- unique(demowas_tidied$otu)
ubiome_demowas <- prune_taxa(taxa_names(ubiome_relative) %in% demowas_otus, ubiome_relative)
demowas_vars <- c("age_group", "Gender", "Ratio_Family_Income_Poverty", "Education_Level", "Marital_Status", "US_Born", "Ethnicity")

demowas_hts    <- build_heatmaps_list(
  phy            = ubiome_demowas,
  vars           = demowas_vars,
  analysis_name  = "DemoWAS",
  genus_mapping  = genus_mapping
)
draw_heatmaps_in_4x4_grid(
  ht_list       = demowas_hts,
  pdf_file_name = "13.1_Log2Mean_GroupComparison_Heatmaps_Demowas.pdf"
)

# ---- 4) OralDisease OTU subset ----
oral_otus   <- unique(oral_d_tidied$otu)
ubiome_oral <- prune_taxa(taxa_names(ubiome_relative) %in% oral_otus, ubiome_relative)
oral_vars   <- unique(oral_d_tidied$phenotype)

oral_hts    <- build_heatmaps_list(
  phy            = ubiome_oral,
  vars           = oral_vars,
  analysis_name  = "OralDisease",
  genus_mapping  = genus_mapping
)
draw_heatmaps_in_4x4_grid(
  ht_list       = oral_hts,
  pdf_file_name = "13.4_Log2Mean_GroupComparison_Heatmaps_OralDisease.pdf"
)
# ---- 5) OutWas OTU subset ----
outwas_otus   <- unique(outwas_sig_res$otu)
ubiome_outwas <- prune_taxa(taxa_names(ubiome_relative) %in% outwas_otus, ubiome_relative)
outwas_vars   <- unique(outwas_sig_res$phenotype)

# Load outwas_sample_data
outwas_sample_data <- readRDS(file.path(intermediate_files_path, "sample_data_outwas.rds"))

# Convert sample_data to a proper data frame and ensure sample IDs are preserved
sd_outwas <- as(sample_data(ubiome_outwas), "data.frame")
original_sample_names <- sample_names(ubiome_outwas)
sd_outwas <- sd_outwas %>%
  mutate(SEQN = original_sample_names)  # Use actual sample names as SEQN

# Merge outwas_sample_data into ubiome_outwas sample_data
sd_outwas <- sd_outwas %>%
  left_join(outwas_sample_data, by = "SEQN") %>%
  # Ensure row names match original sample names
  tibble::column_to_rownames("SEQN") %>%
  .[original_sample_names, , drop = FALSE]  # Reorder to match original sample order

# Update sample_data in ubiome_outwas
sample_data(ubiome_outwas) <- sample_data(sd_outwas)

# Debugging: Print available columns
print("Columns in ubiome_outwas sample_data after merge:")
print(colnames(sample_data(ubiome_outwas)))

outwas_hts    <- build_heatmaps_list(
  phy            = ubiome_outwas,
  vars           = outwas_vars,
  analysis_name  = "OutWas",
  genus_mapping  = genus_mapping
)
draw_heatmaps_in_4x4_grid(
  ht_list       = outwas_hts,
  pdf_file_name = "13.5_Log2Mean_GroupComparison_Heatmaps_OutWas.pdf"
)
```

```{r}
###############################################################################
# FULL CODE: L2FC Heatmap with Custom Threshold Criteria
###############################################################################

#########################
# 0) Libraries & Helpers
#########################
library(phyloseq)       # (https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pcbi.1003531)
library(dplyr)          # (https://doi.org/10.18637/jss.v084.i08)
library(purrr)          # (https://doi.org/10.18637/jss.v070.i10)
library(ComplexHeatmap) # (https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/btw313)
library(circlize)       # (https://doi.org/10.1093/bioinformatics/btu110)
library(grid)           # Base R graphics

save_pdf_figure <- function(file_name, width_mm = 180, height_mm = 170) {
  pdf(
    file       = file_name,
    width      = width_mm / 25.4,
    height     = height_mm / 25.4,
    family     = "Helvetica",
    colormodel = "rgb",
    pointsize  = 5,
    useDingbats= FALSE
  )
}

#########################
# A) Filter OTUs by ≥10% Prevalence
#########################
filter_by_prevalence <- function(phy, threshold = 0.1) {
  if (!inherits(phy, "phyloseq")) {
    stop("`phy` is not a phyloseq object.")
  }
  otu_mat <- as(otu_table(phy), "matrix")
  if (!taxa_are_rows(phy)) {
    otu_mat <- t(otu_mat)
  }
  n_samples <- ncol(otu_mat)
  keep <- rowSums(otu_mat > 0) / n_samples >= threshold
  prune_taxa(keep, phy)
}

###############################################################################
# B) Safe Tertile/Quartile Cut Helpers
#    If breaks are not unique, fallback to a median (binary) split
###############################################################################
safe_cut_tertiles <- function(x_num) {
  ps <- quantile(x_num, probs = c(0, 1/3, 2/3, 1), na.rm = TRUE)
  # fallback if breaks are not unique
  if (length(unique(ps)) < 4) {
    med_val <- median(x_num, na.rm = TRUE)
    f <- ifelse(x_num <= med_val, "Low", "High")
    return(factor(f, levels = c("Low","High")))
  }
  labs <- c("T1","T2","T3")
  cut(x_num, breaks = ps, include.lowest = TRUE, labels = labs)
}

safe_cut_quartiles <- function(x_num) {
  qs <- quantile(x_num, probs = c(0,0.25,0.5,0.75,1), na.rm = TRUE)
  if (length(unique(qs)) < 5) {
    med_val <- median(x_num, na.rm = TRUE)
    f <- ifelse(x_num <= med_val, "Low", "High")
    return(factor(f, levels = c("Low","High")))
  }
  labs <- c("Q1","Q2","Q3","Q4")
  cut(x_num, breaks = qs, include.lowest = TRUE, labels = labs)
}

###############################################################################
# C) A Map from variable names to more descriptive labels
###############################################################################
variable_label_map <- c(
  # EXPO
  DS1DSCNT = "Dietary Supplements (#)",
  LBX2DF   = "Blood 2,5-Dimethylfuran (ng/mL)",
  LBXBCD   = "Blood Cadmium (µg/L)",
  LBXCOT   = "Cotinine (ng/mL)",
  LBXV4E   = "Blood 1,1,2,2-Tetrachloroethane (ng/mL)",
  LBXVBZ   = "Blood Benzene (ng/mL)",
  LBXVCT   = "Blood Carbon Tetrachloride (ng/mL)",
  LBXVFN   = "Blood Furan (ng/mL)",
  LBXVHE   = "Blood Hexachloroethane (ng/mL)",
  URXNAL   = "Urinary Total NNAL (ng/mL)",

  # PHENO
  ENXMEAN  = "FENO (ppb)",
  LBDLYMNO = "Lymphocyte Count (1000/µL)",
  LBDNENO  = "Neutrophils (1000/µL)",
  LBXHCT   = "Hematocrit (%)",
  LBXMCHSI = "Mean Cell Hemoglobin (pg)",
  LBXMCVSI = "Mean Cell Volume (fL)",
  LBXSBU   = "Blood Urea Nitrogen (mg/dL)",
  LBXSGB   = "Globulin (g/dL)",
  LBXSUA   = "Uric Acid (mg/dL)",
  LBXWBCSI = "WBC Count (x10³/µL)",

  # ORAL
  DENTURE_OHAROCDE      = "Denture Status",
  GUM_DISEASE_OHAROCGP  = "Gum Disease",
  ORAL_HYGIENE_OHAROCOH = "Oral Hygiene",
  TOOTH_DECAY_OHAROCDT  = "Tooth Decay"
)

###############################################################################
# D) Custom Recode Function
#    Applies threshold logic from your corrected tables
###############################################################################
recode_variable <- function(x, var_name, sex = NULL) {
  x_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x))

  # 1) EXAMPLE EXPOSURE-RELATED RECODINGS
  if (var_name == "DS1DSCNT") {
    # Binary: 0 vs. ≥1
    f <- ifelse(x_num < 1, "0", ">=1")
    return(factor(f, levels = c("0",">=1")))
  }
  if (var_name == "LBX2DF") {
    # quartiles
    return(safe_cut_quartiles(x_num))
  }
  if (var_name == "LBXBCD") {
    # <0.5 vs >=0.5
    f <- ifelse(x_num < 0.5, "<0.5", ">=0.5")
    return(factor(f, levels = c("<0.5",">=0.5")))
  }
  if (var_name == "LBXCOT") {
    # <3, 3–10, >10
    f <- cut(x_num, breaks = c(-Inf, 3, 10, Inf),
             labels = c("<3","3–10",">10"), right = TRUE)
    return(f)
  }
  if (var_name == "LBXV4E") {
    # median split
    med_val <- median(x_num, na.rm = TRUE)
    f <- ifelse(x_num <= med_val, "Low", "High")
    return(factor(f, levels = c("Low","High")))
  }
  if (var_name == "LBXVBZ") {
    # tertiles
    return(safe_cut_tertiles(x_num))
  }
  if (var_name == "LBXVCT") {
    # 75th percentile split
    p75 <- quantile(x_num, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE)
    f <- ifelse(x_num <= p75, "Low", "High")
    return(factor(f, levels = c("Low","High")))
  }
  if (var_name == "LBXVFN") {
    # Tertiles
    return(safe_cut_tertiles(x_num))
  }
  if (var_name == "LBXVHE") {
    # median split
    med_val <- median(x_num, na.rm = TRUE)
    f <- ifelse(x_num <= med_val, "Low", "High")
    return(factor(f, levels = c("Low","High")))
  }
  if (var_name == "URXNAL") {
    # <0.1, >=0.1
    f <- ifelse(x_num < 0.1, "<0.1", ">=0.1")
    return(factor(f, levels = c("<0.1",">=0.1")))
  }

  # 2) EXAMPLE PHENOTYPE-RELATED RECODINGS
  if (var_name == "ENXMEAN") {
    # <25, 25–50, >50
    f <- cut(x_num, breaks = c(-Inf, 25, 50, Inf),
             labels = c("<25","25–50",">50"), right = TRUE)
    return(f)
  }
  if (var_name == "LBDLYMNO") {
    # <1, 1–4, >4
    f <- cut(x_num, breaks = c(-Inf, 1, 4, Inf),
             labels = c("<1","1–4",">4"), right = TRUE)
    return(f)
  }
  if (var_name == "LBDNENO") {
    # <2, 2–7, >7
    f <- cut(x_num, breaks = c(-Inf, 2, 7, Inf),
             labels = c("<2","2–7",">7"), right = TRUE)
    return(f)
  }
  if (var_name == "LBXHCT") {
    # <37 = Low, 37–52 = Normal, >52 = High
    f <- cut(x_num, breaks = c(-Inf, 37, 52, Inf),
             labels = c("Low","Normal","High"), right = TRUE)
    return(f)
  }
  if (var_name == "LBXMCHSI") {
    # <27, 27–33, >33
    f <- cut(x_num, breaks = c(-Inf, 27, 33, Inf),
             labels = c("<27","27–33",">33"), right = TRUE)
    return(f)
  }
  if (var_name == "LBXMCVSI") {
    # Micro, Normal, Macro
    f <- cut(x_num, breaks = c(-Inf,80,100,Inf),
             labels = c("Micro","Normal","Macro"), right = TRUE)
    return(f)
  }
  if (var_name == "LBXSBU") {
    # <7, 7–20, >20
    f <- cut(x_num, breaks = c(-Inf, 7, 20, Inf),
             labels = c("<7","7–20",">20"), right = TRUE)
    return(f)
  }
  if (var_name == "LBXSGB") {
    # <2, 2–3.5, >3.5
    f <- cut(x_num, breaks = c(-Inf, 2, 3.5, Inf),
             labels = c("<2","2–3.5",">3.5"), right = TRUE)
    return(f)
  }
  if (var_name == "LBXSUA") {
    # Males >7 mg/dL, Females >6 mg/dL. If sex unknown => 7 mg/dL
    if (!is.null(sex)) {
      x_cut <- ifelse(sex == "M", 7, 6)
      f <- ifelse(x_num > x_cut, "High", "Normal")
    } else {
      f <- ifelse(x_num > 7, "High", "Normal")
    }
    return(factor(f, levels = c("Normal","High")))
  }
  if (var_name == "LBXWBCSI") {
    # <4, 4–11, >11
    f <- cut(x_num, breaks = c(-Inf, 4, 11, Inf),
             labels = c("<4","4–11",">11"), right = TRUE)
    return(f)
  }

  # 3) EXAMPLE ORAL VARIABLES
  if (var_name == "DENTURE_OHAROCDE") {
    f <- factor(ifelse(as.numeric(x) == 1, "Yes", "No"), levels = c("No","Yes"))
    return(f)
  }
  if (var_name == "GUM_DISEASE_OHAROCGP") {
    f <- factor(ifelse(as.numeric(x) == 1, "Yes", "No"), levels = c("No","Yes"))
    return(f)
  }
  if (var_name == "ORAL_HYGIENE_OHAROCOH") {
    f <- factor(ifelse(as.numeric(x) == 1, "Good", "Poor/Fair"), levels = c("Poor/Fair","Good"))
    return(f)
  }
  if (var_name == "TOOTH_DECAY_OHAROCDT") {
    f <- factor(ifelse(as.numeric(x) == 1, "Yes", "No"), levels = c("No","Yes"))
    return(f)
  }

  # Fallback: treat as factor with no recoding
  factor(as.character(x))
}

#########################
# E) Compute L2FC for One Variable
#########################
compute_l2fc_for_variable <- function(phy, var_name) {
  sd <- as.data.frame(sample_data(phy))
  if (!var_name %in% colnames(sd)) {
    message("Variable '", var_name, "' not in sample_data(). Skipping.")
    return(NULL)
  }

  f <- recode_variable(sd[[var_name]], var_name = var_name)
  if (length(levels(f)) < 2) {
    return(NULL)
  }

  sd[[var_name]] <- f
  sample_data(phy) <- phyloseq::sample_data(sd)

  otu_mat <- as(otu_table(phy), "matrix")
  if (!taxa_are_rows(phy)) {
    otu_mat <- t(otu_mat)
  }
  ref_level    <- levels(f)[1]
  other_levels <- levels(f)[-1]
  if (!length(other_levels)) return(NULL)

  out_list <- list()
  for (lv in other_levels) {
    idx_ref <- which(f == ref_level)
    idx_tre <- which(f == lv)
    if (!length(idx_ref) || !length(idx_tre)) next
  
    mean_ref <- rowMeans(otu_mat[, idx_ref, drop = FALSE], na.rm = TRUE)
    mean_tre <- rowMeans(otu_mat[, idx_tre, drop = FALSE], na.rm = TRUE)
    l2fc_vec <- log2((mean_tre + 1e-10) / (mean_ref + 1e-10))
  
    # -- NEW: store reference in the col_name
    col_name <- paste0(var_name, "_", lv, "_REF_", ref_level)
    out_list[[col_name]] <- l2fc_vec
  }
  

  if (!length(out_list)) return(NULL)
  df_out <- as.data.frame(out_list)
  rownames(df_out) <- rownames(otu_mat)
  df_out
}

#########################
# F) Build L2FC matrix for a list of variables
#########################
build_l2fc_matrix <- function(phy, var_list) {
  all_cols <- list()
  for (v in var_list) {
    df_v <- compute_l2fc_for_variable(phy, v)
    if (!is.null(df_v)) {
      # Instead of: all_cols[[v]] <- df_v
      # Use an un‐named index:
      all_cols[[ length(all_cols) + 1 ]] <- df_v
    }
  }
  if (!length(all_cols)) {
    message("No L2FC columns found for these vars.")
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Now do.call(cbind, all_cols) will not artificially insert
  # 'VAR.VAR_' in your column names:
  do.call(cbind, all_cols)
}


#########################
# G) Column Annotation + Descriptive Column Names
#########################

# 1) Modify the descriptive_colname to use parentheses for factor level
###############################################################################
# More Readable Column Labels
# Updated snippet for the "descriptive_colname" step
###############################################################################

parse_factor_label <- function(var_label, factor_lvl) {
  # Attempt to extract any unit in parentheses from var_label
  unit <- sub(".*\\(([^)]*)\\).*", "\\1", var_label)
  if (identical(unit, var_label)) {
    unit <- ""
  } else {
    var_label <- sub("\\s*\\([^)]*\\)\\s*", "", var_label)
  }

  # Fix only exact >= or <=
  factor_lvl <- gsub(">=", "≥", factor_lvl, fixed = TRUE)
  factor_lvl <- gsub("<=", "≤", factor_lvl, fixed = TRUE)

  # If factor_lvl has numeric text and we found a unit, append
  has_number <- grepl("[0-9]", factor_lvl)
  if (has_number && nzchar(unit)) {
    factor_lvl <- paste0(factor_lvl, " ", unit)
  }

  paste0(var_label, ": ", factor_lvl)
}


# 2) Overhaul descriptive_colname to rely on parse_factor_label
descriptive_colname <- function(col_name) {
  # Example: col_name = "LBXBCD_>=0.5_REF_<0.5"

  # 1) Split off the "_REF_" part, so:
  #    splittedREF[1] = "LBXBCD_>=0.5"
  #    splittedREF[2] = "<0.5"
  splittedREF <- strsplit(col_name, "_REF_")[[1]]
  main_part   <- splittedREF[1]
  ref_part    <- if (length(splittedREF) > 1) splittedREF[2] else NA

  # 2) The main part has varName and factorLvl, e.g. "LBXBCD_>=0.5"
  pieces    <- strsplit(main_part, "_")[[1]]
  varName   <- pieces[1]                          # e.g., "LBXBCD"
  factorLvl <- paste0(pieces[-1], collapse = "_") # e.g., ">=0.5"

  # 3) Look up descriptive label for the variable
  descVar <- variable_label_map[varName]
  if (is.na(descVar)) {
    descVar <- varName  # fallback
  }

  # 4) Convert the main factor level to a descriptive label
  #    e.g. "Blood Cadmium: ≥0.5 µg/L"
  main_label <- parse_factor_label(descVar, factorLvl)

  # 5) If ref_part exists, parse that also to build bracket text
  #    e.g. "Blood Cadmium: <0.5 µg/L" => we only want the factor portion
  if (!is.na(ref_part)) {
    ref_label_full <- parse_factor_label(descVar, ref_part)
    # ref_label_full might be "Blood Cadmium: <0.5 µg/L"
    # We'll isolate the substring after the colon
    splitted2     <- strsplit(ref_label_full, ": ", fixed = TRUE)[[1]]
    if (length(splitted2) >= 2) {
      ref_label_part <- splitted2[2]  # e.g. "<0.5 µg/L"
    } else {
      ref_label_part <- splitted2[1]  # fallback
    }
    # Combine: e.g. "Blood Cadmium: ≥0.5 µg/L [vs <0.5 µg/L as ref]"
    final_label <- paste0(main_label, " [vs ", ref_label_part, " as ref]")
  } else {
    # No REF_ part => just return main_label
    final_label <- main_label
  }

  final_label
}


###############################################################################
# Then in your "produce_l2fc_heatmap" function, you don't change anything else.
# The only difference is that "descriptive_colname" now yields more readable text.
###############################################################################


# 2) Column annotation by variable name
make_column_annotation <- function(col_names) {
  varname_vec <- sapply(col_names, function(x) strsplit(x,"_")[[1]][1])
  varname_vec <- factor(varname_vec)
  var_levels  <- levels(varname_vec)

  # color palette for variables
  col_palette <- scales::hue_pal()(length(var_levels))
  names(col_palette) <- var_levels

  HeatmapAnnotation(
    Variable = varname_vec,
    col = list(Variable = col_palette),
    annotation_legend_param = list(
      Variable = list(title = "Variable")
    )
  )
}

#########################
# H) Produce & Save Single Heatmap
#########################
produce_l2fc_heatmap <- function(phy,
                                 vars,
                                 pdf_name,
                                 top_n      = 50,
                                 min_val    = -3,
                                 max_val    = 3,
                                 width_mm   = 180,   # <-- pass these from your call
                                 height_mm  = 120) { # <--
  # 1) build L2FC
  l2fc_mat <- build_l2fc_matrix(phy, vars)
  if (is.null(l2fc_mat)) {
    message("Skipping heatmap: No L2FC for '", pdf_name, "'")
    return(NULL)
  }

  # 2) add OTU column
  df_mat <- as.data.frame(l2fc_mat)
  df_mat[["otu"]] <- rownames(df_mat)

  # 3) map OTU -> Genus
  genus_mapping <- data.frame(
    otu   = rownames(tax_table(ubiome_relative)),
    Genus = as.character(tax_table(ubiome_relative)[,"Genus"]),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )

  df_joined <- df_mat %>%
    left_join(genus_mapping, by = "otu") %>%
    mutate(
      Genus = if_else(
        is.na(Genus) | Genus == "unclassified",
        paste0("NA; ", gsub("^RSV_|^OTU_", "", otu)),  # Remove RSV_ or OTU_
        Genus  # Keep original genus name
      )
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      Genus = str_remove_all(Genus, "group|_relative")  # Remove "group" and "_relative"
    ) %>%
    mutate(row_label = Genus)  # Use cleaned Genus as row label

  # 4) pick top N by max abs
  l2fc_cols <- setdiff(colnames(df_joined), c("otu","Genus","row_label"))
  if (!length(l2fc_cols)) {
    message("No L2FC columns in final data for '", pdf_name, "'.")
    return(NULL)
  }
  df_joined[["max_abs"]] <- apply(df_joined[l2fc_cols], 1,
                                  function(x) max(abs(x), na.rm = TRUE))
  top_df <- df_joined %>% arrange(desc(max_abs)) %>% head(top_n)

  final_mat <- as.matrix(top_df[l2fc_cols])
  rownames(final_mat) <- top_df[["row_label"]]

  # 5) column annotation
  col_ha <- make_column_annotation(colnames(final_mat))

  # 6) rename columns
  new_colnames <- sapply(colnames(final_mat), descriptive_colname)
  colnames(final_mat) <- new_colnames

  # 7) color scale
  col_fun <- circlize::colorRamp2(c(min_val, 0, max_val),
                                  c("blue","white","red"))

  # 8) build & save heatmap
  ht <- Heatmap(
    final_mat,
    name            = "L2FC",
    col             = col_fun,
    cluster_rows    = TRUE,
    cluster_columns = TRUE,
    # top_annotation  = col_ha,
```
